summary: Formation: present tense stem + endings — ty: -Ø/-j, my: -me/-jme, vy: -te/-jte
Type 1 (-ať verbs, 3sg -á): add -j/-jme/-jte — čítaj! čítajme! čítajte!
Type 2 (-iť verbs, 3sg -í): bare stem/-me/-te — rob! robme! robte!
Type 3 (-ovať verbs, 3sg -uje): add -j/-jme/-jte — pracuj! pracujme! pracujte!
Reflexive sa/si comes AFTER the imperative: uč sa! učme sa! učte sa!
Negation: ne- on the verb — nerob! nerobme! nerobte!
Perfective = single completed command (Napíš!), Imperfective = general/repeated (Píš!)
Key irregulars: buď (be), choď (go), poď (come), jedz (eat), pi (drink), daj (give), vezmi (take)
Soften with prosím (please) or use môžeš/mohol by si for polite requests

rules.title: The Three Imperative Forms
rules.text: Slovak imperative has three forms based on who you're commanding. All are built from the present tense stem.
examples: Robme to spolu! – Let's do it together!
Robte to rýchlo! – Do it quickly! (formal/plural)

rules.title: Formation by Verb Type
rules.text: The imperative ending depends on the conjugation type. If the 3rd person singular ends in a vowel (-á, -uje), add -j. If it ends in a consonant (-í, -ie), use the bare stem.

rules.title: Perfective vs Imperfective Imperative
rules.text: Perfective = command to complete one specific action. Imperfective = general instruction, repeated action, or ongoing behavior.
examples: Píš mi často! – Write to me often! (repeated → imperfective)
Napíš mi zajtra! – Write to me tomorrow! (one action → perfective)
Čítaj každý deň! – Read every day! (habitual → imperfective)
Prečítaj túto knihu! – Read this book through! (complete → perfective)

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs and Negation
rules.text: Reflexive sa/si always comes AFTER the imperative form. Negation adds ne- to the front of the verb. Both can combine.
examples: Neuč sa to! – Don't learn that!

rules.title: Irregular Imperatives
rules.text: These common verbs have unpredictable imperative forms. Must be memorized.
examples: Daj mi to! – Give it to me!

rules.title: Making Requests Polite
rules.text: Add prosím (please) before or after the imperative. For even softer requests, use môžeš (can you) or the conditional mohol by si (could you).
examples: Majte sa dobre! – Take care! (formal farewell)

vocab.en: to stand up, to get up
to look forward to, to be glad
to sign up, to log in
